name: libalgorithm-merge-perl
title: libalgorithm-merge-perl
summary: Perl module for three-way merge of textual data
description:
Perl module for three-way merge of textual data
Algorithm::Merge provides three-way merge and diff functions, complementing
the functionality offered by Algorithm::Diff (libalgorithm-diff-perl). Given
three sets of items, known as the original, left and right, this module can
take a three-way difference or merge them. Taking a difference provides an
array reference that is very similar to the behaviour of Algorithm::Diff. One
can also implement custom conflict resolution using the CONFLICT callback.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libalgorithm-merge-perl because Perl module for three-way merge of textual data.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libalgorithm-merge-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libalgorithm-merge-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libalgorithm-merge-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
